q: Which best describes a negative feedback loop in homeostasis?
options: A change in a variable triggers a response that amplifies the original change further
The body detects a deviation from the set point and initiates a response that reverses the deviation
Hormones continuously increase their effect regardless of the detected variable level
The nervous system shuts down all effectors when the body reaches its set point
explanation: Negative feedback detects deviation from a set point and activates corrective mechanisms that oppose the change, returning the variable to the set point.
